english: You carried the Vedas in the waters of the final deluge, your boat-like acts effortlessly done
roman: Pralaaya payodhi jale dhrita vaanasi Vedam vihita vahitra charitram akhedam

english: O Keshava who assumed the fish form — victory to the Lord of the universe, O Hari
roman: Keshava dhrita mina sharira jaya jagadisha Hare

english: The earth rests on your vast back, hard as a quiver from bearing the world
roman: Kshitir ati vipula lasta va tishthati prushthe dharani dharana kina chakra garishtham

english: O Keshava who assumed the tortoise form — victory to the Lord of the universe
roman: Keshava dhrita kachchhapa rupa jaya jagadisha Hare

hindi: नरहरि दरहर सरोजमुखमनिशं, दरहर पयोधर निजकर सरोजम्।
english: O Narahari, constantly bear the lotus-face, holding the ocean of milk in your own lotus hands
roman: Nara hari dara hara saroja mukham anisham dara hara payo dhara nija kara sarojam

english: O Keshava who assumed the man-lion form — victory to the Lord of the universe
roman: Keshava dhrita nara hari rupa jaya jagadisha Hare

hindi: श्री जयदेवकवेरिदमुदितमुदारं, शृणु सुखदं शुभदं भवसारम्।
english: This composition of the poet Jayadeva is noble — hear it, it gives happiness, auspiciousness and the essence of existence
roman: Shri Jayadeva kaveir idam uditam udaram shrinu sukhad am shubhadam bhava saram

hindi: केशव धृत दशविधरूप जय जगदीश हरे॥
english: O Keshava who assumed ten forms — victory to the Lord of the universe
roman: Keshava dhrita dasha vidha rupa jaya jagadisha Hare
